title: bahiopsis carterae
intro: Bahiopsis carterae, also known as the pink daisy bush, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's flowers and leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Bahiopsis carterae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
